label: fairness_constrained_safety_rule
description: In unavoidable-harm scenarios, choose the maneuver that (1) keeps predicted occupant injury within a specified safety ceiling and (2) never selects an option whose expected injuries for any external road-user class exceed a defined “harm-shift” limit versus the best alternative, then among remaining candidates (3) minimize total expected injuries.

label: constrained_harm_minimization
description: In unavoidable-harm scenarios, the vehicle chooses the collision outcome that minimizes total expected injuries (Policy 1 objective) while enforcing severity-weighted residual-risk bounds so harm distribution across road-user categories and geographies cannot systematically drift toward specific vulnerable groups.

label: context_based_dynamic_risk_balancing
description: Autonomous emergency vehicles will follow a hybrid approach that dynamically adjusts harm distribution based on real-time factors.

label: constrained_harm_minimization
description: Optimize as in Policy 1 (minimize total expected injuries), but add enforceable constraints that limit disparities in expected unavoidable-harm per exposure across road-user groups.

label: adaptive_equity_protocol
description: A protocol that distributes risk evenly while dynamically adjusting for the unique vulnerabilities of different road users.

label: constrained_harm_minimization
description: Optimize for minimum total expected injuries subject to hard equity guardrails and occupant constraints.

label: dynamic_ethical_scaling
description: This approach combines fairness, urgency, and harm minimization by programming vehicles to adapt risk distribution based on situational context.

label: occupant_safety_floor
description: Use a constrained optimization rule: restrict to only those trajectories whose predicted occupant injury severity is below a city-specified safety floor, and within that feasible set choose the option that minimizes total expected injuries across all road users.

label: adaptive_risk_management_protocols
description: This policy incorporates equity and fairness in risk distribution while allowing for ongoing evaluation and adjustments based on real-world outcomes.

label: context_aware_dynamic_priority_system
description: This policy would equip autonomous emergency vehicles with a dynamic decision-making system that adjusts risk distribution based on contextual factors.

label: injury_minimizing_fairness_caps
description: In unavoidable-harm events, select the maneuver that minimizes total expected injuries while enforcing robust, auditable caps on residual severe-injury risk disparity across predefined road-user groups and corridor exposure over rolling windows.

label: harm_min_with_equity_caps
description: Use Policy 1 (minimize total expected injuries) as the primary objective, but add hard constraints that cap how much expected residual risk (or injury rate) any road-user group or geographic corridor may exceed a City-defined baseline—enforced and recalibrated via scheduled audits.

label: context_sensitive_risk_distribution
description: A dynamic approach that balances fairness and harm minimization based on specific circumstances of each unavoidable-harm scenario.

label: contextual_risk_balancing
description: A hybrid policy that dynamically prioritizes harm reduction based on contextual factors, such as the vulnerability and urgency of those involved in the scenario.

label: patient_plus_fairness_constraints
description: In unavoidable-harm scenarios, the vehicle must enforce hard caps on acceptable patient injury severity/probability and then choose among the remaining feasible actions to minimize total expected injuries while adding a fairness constraint that prevents systematic residual-harm concentration across defined road-user groups.

label: constrained_residual_equality
description: In unavoidable-harm scenarios, the vehicle chooses the maneuver that minimizes total expected injuries subject to an auditable cap on residual-risk disparity across exposure-defined road-user groups, with minimum protection thresholds for vulnerable groups.

label: context_based_risk_balancing
description: A tiered decision-making system that prioritizes safety based on the context and vulnerability of those involved in unavoidable-harm scenarios.

label: context_aware_ethical_prioritization
description: This approach involves programming the autonomous vehicles to assess specific situational factors in unavoidable-harm scenarios and make decisions based on a weighted combination of principles prioritizing vulnerability, minimizing harm, and ensuring equity.

label: fairness_capped_harm_minimization
description: Use an optimization policy that minimizes total expected injuries subject to strict upper bounds (caps) on expected harm by road-user group and corridor, and with lexicographic override so no feasible decision may increase harm in any capped group beyond its threshold even if it would reduce total injuries elsewhere.

label: weighted_vulnerability_framework
description: Design the autonomous emergency vehicle’s decision-making system to prioritize minimizing harm while accounting for the unique vulnerabilities of specific groups.

label: patient_first_harm_caps
description: Use a lexicographic rule that first minimizes harm to the vehicle occupants, but only among options that also satisfy hard maximum external-harm constraints.

label: fairness_with_context
description: A hybrid approach that prioritizes equity while allowing for contextual sensitivity in decision-making.

label: fairness_constrained_unavoidable_harm
description: Recommend a fairness-constrained 'unavoidable-harm' rule that minimizes total expected injuries subject to explicit constraints.

label: contextual_fairness_framework
description: A hybrid approach that adjusts the vehicle's decision-making algorithm based on context while addressing broader systemic vulnerabilities.

label: context_sensitive_harm_minimization
description: A system that prioritizes vehicle occupants while actively minimizing harm to all road users through context-aware strategies.

label: equity_constrained_harm_minimization
description: Recommend a primary objective of minimizing total expected injuries in unavoidable-harm scenarios, subject to hard, exposure-weighted guardrails that cap disparity in residual risk across road-user groups/locations, with mandatory post-deployment audits and constraint updates.

label: context_aware_risk_distribution
description: This policy combines fairness with dynamic adjustments to risk distribution based on the context of emergencies.

label: duty_bound_fairness_constraints
description: Recommend an optimization policy that guarantees occupants are protected to a defined safety threshold while minimizing expected harm subject to bounded disparities in residual risk across road-user classes/locations.

label: worst_case_harm_minimax
description: In unavoidable-harm scenarios, choose the vehicle behavior that minimizes the maximum injury severity across all road-user groups, using expected total injuries only as a tie-breaker.

label: context_aware_ethical_framework
description: A system that adapts its decision-making based on the specific circumstances of each unavoidable-harm scenario.

label: proactive_harm_prevention
description: This policy would distribute residual risk evenly across all road users while committing to infrastructure improvements and proactive harm-reduction measures.

label: patient_protection_harm_caps
description: In unavoidable-harm cases, set the primary objective to maximize the probability of avoiding severe injury to the occupants, subject to hard, pre-published caps on expected harm to each outside road-user group and corridor over time, using uncertainty-robust estimates and ongoing public reporting.

label: patient_first_harm_caps
description: Require that, in unavoidable-harm scenarios, the vehicle first selects actions that keep total expected harm to outside road users below predefined hard thresholds, and only then minimize injury risk to the patient/occupants within those constraints, with independent public auditing and 'reduce-unavoidable' triggers.

label: context_aware_weighted_prioritization

label: exposure_capped_occupant_priority
description: Use an occupants-first objective for unavoidable-harm decisions, but impose hard, exposure-adjusted caps on incremental residual risk to any external road-user subgroup/corridor, and only if the caps would be violated fall back to an overall harm-minimization rule.

label: context_adaptive_ethics
description: A policy where the autonomous system dynamically evaluates each unavoidable-harm scenario based on urgency, vulnerability, and fairness.

label: occupant_safety_guardrails
description: In unavoidable-harm scenarios, the ambulance control chooses the action that maximizes expected survival/health of vehicle occupants while enforcing hard, pre-committed caps on expected harm to each external road-user group and corridor.

label: patient_first_equity_caps
description: In unavoidable-harm scenarios, the vehicle controller first selects maneuvers that minimize harm to the vehicle’s occupants (lexicographically patient-first), and then—among those occupant-safest options—chooses the action that minimizes the maximum residual injury risk to any external road-user group, with pre-set caps to prevent predictable offloading into 'hotspot' populations.

label: context_aware_prioritization
description: This policy prioritizes safety dynamically based on the specific vulnerabilities of those involved in an unavoidable-harm scenario.

label: adaptive_risk_mitigation
description: A policy that combines fairness, efficiency, and urgency through real-time data and machine learning to dynamically assess and balance harm distribution in unavoidable-harm scenarios.

label: constrained_patient_first_fairness
description: Use a lexicographic, constrained decision rule that first prioritizes preventing severe harm to the vehicle occupants, but only among maneuvers that keep every external road-user class within predefined harm-rate and equity bounds, and then minimizes total expected injuries.

label: fairness_plus

label: constrained_expected_harm_equity
description: Use Policy 1 as the primary objective, but add exposure-weighted equity constraints to cap risk concentration in any road-user category.

label: constrained_fair_optimization
description: Minimize total expected injuries subject to pre-specified, auditable constraints that limit how concentrated residual risk can be across road-user classes and location/time bands, using robust uncertainty margins and publishing both totals and distribution metrics for council review.

label: dual_track_equity_policy

label: harm_minimize_with_patient_caps

label: risk_capped_optimization
description: Use Policy 1’s core objective but add explicit, exposure-adjusted group/routing constraints to cap harm across road-user groups.

label: equity_constrained_minimization
description: Use a constrained-optimization rule for unavoidable-harm events that minimizes total expected injuries subject to hard caps on disparity of residual risk across road-user categories and city zones.

label: context_based_risk_balancing
description: A dynamic approach prioritizing risk mitigation based on contextual factors such as the vulnerability of occupants and other road users.

label: dynamic_vulnerability_prioritization
description: Design the system to assess and prioritize protection for the most vulnerable individuals in each unique scenario.

label: context_sensitive_risk_distribution
description: A policy that dynamically adjusts harm allocation based on the specific circumstances of each unavoidable-harm event.

label: constrained_residual_fairness
description: Adopt harm-minimization for unavoidable-harm decisions, but only among options whose resulting residual risk to exposure-defined road-user groups stays within a pre-set fairness bound, with mandatory public auditing and correction triggers.

label: patient_first_fairness_cap
description: In unavoidable-harm scenarios, the system first chooses the maneuver that minimizes injury to the vehicle’s occupants, but only among those options that keep residual risk to each external road-user group within a predefined fairness bound.

label: participate_with_safeguards
description: Implement ongoing capacity reassessments and participant safeguards while involving family in the decision-making process.

label: equity_capped_optimization
description: Minimize total expected injuries under an ex-ante constraint that severe-harm risk for each road-user group must stay within pre-set bounds.

label: context_adaptive_risk_balancing
description: This policy would employ advanced algorithms that prioritize fairness by default but adjust dynamically based on the unique vulnerabilities present in each scenario.
